source_types.name: Utility / DNO Transformer
source_types.notes: Standard utility-side source. Constant EMF, no decrement. Building services projects typically use this.

source_types.name: Synchronous Generator (Standby / Cogen)
source_types.notes: Standby gen or cogen unit. Subtransient X"d first cycle. Decrement factor μ at breaking time.

source_types.name: UPS / Battery Inverter
source_types.iec_reference: Not directly in IEC 60909-0; IEC 62040-3 + manufacturer data
source_types.notes: Output current limited by IGBT/IGCT. Transitions to bypass during sustained fault.

source_types.name: Induction Motor Aggregate Back-Feed
source_types.notes: Running motors contribute via subtransient reactance for 1-2 cycles. 2016 amendment lowered threshold from 5% to 1%.

_schema_field_mapping.description: Source-type ids in this ontology match the `project_supply.lv_source.type` IR enum (long form: utility_transformer/generator/ups/mixed). The narrower `sources[].kind` IR enum (short form: utility/generator/ups/motor_aggregate) uses the abbreviated 'utility' for the same type — `motor_aggregate` is a sources[]-only kind for the IEC 60909 motor back-feed contribution. Documented post-Reviewer-1 round-2 (the apparent mismatch is a deliberate dual-form: longer descriptive form at the supply-arrangement field, shorter contributor form at the multi-source field).
_schema_field_mapping.cross_reference: ontology.id == lv_source.type for utility_transformer/generator/ups; sources[].kind 'utility' is the short-form alias for utility_transformer at the contributor slot
